event: no close contact with infected individuals
treated with hepatic puncture and drainage
liver and renal function tests slightly abnormal
serum glutamic oxalacetic transaminase level of 67 U/L
total protein and albumin levels of 50.8 and 21.5 g/L
blood urea nitrogen level of 10.8 mmol/L
right abdominal tenderness without rebound or guarding
drainage catheter yield of 150 mL of fulvous fluid
abdominal and pelvic computed tomography (CT) scan
irregular, slightly low-density lesion in the right posterior hepatic lobe
liquid density shadow and high-density drainage tube shadow
round-like low-density lesion of about 0.8 cm in diameter
appendix thickened to about 20 mm in diameter
structure of the ascending colon near the ileocecal region became disorganised
multiple gas accumulation and dilation in the bowel
fulvous purulent exudate and necrotic tissue filled the extraperitoneal space and abdominal cavity
partial postnecrotic defect in the peritoneum
massive epiploon adhesion in the right upper abdomen
one perforation under the right side of the liver
other perforation in the right ascending colon
partial resection of the ascending colon
drainage of hepatic, abdominal and extraperitoneal abscesses
transferred to the intensive care unit (ICU)
nutritional support therapy and blood transfusion were also used
blood cultures and drainage fluids were sent
empirically started on intravenous tigecycline and piperacillin/tazobactam
body temperature, routine blood count, procalcitonin level and C-reactive protein (CRP) level were closely monitored dynamically
electrocardiogram monitor showed a pulse rate of 130 beats/minute
blood samples were taken for aerobic and anaerobic cultures
positive anaerobic blood culture vials drawn postoperatively
Gram stain revealed short Gram-positive bacillus without spores
positive culture broth was inoculated onto blood agar base medium and anaerobically incubated for three more days
colony from the agar plate was identified as E. lenta by matrix-assisted laser desorption/ionization time-of-flight mass spectrometry (MALDI-TOF MS)
antimicrobial susceptibility testing of E. lenta was not performed
cultures of the drainage fluid were obtained
isolation of organism as Escherichia coli
patient was treated with ertapenem and teicoplanin
fever, leukocytosis, PCT level and CRP level promptly improved
patient was transferred to the general ward after 3 days
patient was administered ertapenem and teicoplanin for another 14 days
repeated blood cultures after the introduction of antibiotics were negative
repeat CT revealed that the size of the hepatic abscess and the amount of ascites decreased
discharged from the hospital with a prescription for oral antibiotics (clindamycin)
remained well as an outpatient without a recurrence of fever
complete resolution of the abscess was found at follow-up with a six-week course of intravenous ertapenem
